conbody.p: Overview: The Encounter and Suggestion Details dashboard provides encounters details to the CDI Manager, displaying information on their assigned rank, AI suggestions, CDS activity, and suggested DRG. This report helps to analyze the data and identify opportunities for improvement.
By default, the report displays data for last 1 calendar month based on the admission date.
Navigation: On the Analytics home page left side panel, navigate to CDE One Triage and CDS Assistant Usage Encounter and Suggestion Details .

The Encounter and Suggestion Details report displays the following:
KPI Card: Total Suggestions, Total Encounters
Encounter Details chart
Suggestion Details chart

li.p: Encounters that have been given a rank in CDE Triage are called ranked encounters. The current rank of an encounter may be blank.
Encounters are ranked based on the rules that are defined for the prioritization within CDE Triage.

ul.li:
The following encounter types are considered:
Both ranked (standard and custom) and unranked encounters.
Highest rank of the encounter.
Count of all encounters that have been assigned a rank and have an initial review date/time.
All encounter statuses except 'Review Not Needed' are considered.

conbody.p: On the top panel, you can select desired values for the slicers (refer ) and the corresponding data gets generated in the tabular chart.
For filters on all pages, refer .

p.b: Encounter and Suggestion Details - KPI Cards

row.entry:
The total count of suggestions with all statuses including Review Not Needed for the reporting period.
Suggestion could be Documentation Opportunities Diagnoses, Documented Diagnoses, Documented Procedures, Clinical Evidence.

entry.p: The total count of encounters with all statuses including Review Not Needed for the reporting period.When the Admit Date is applied, then this card displays the count of admitted encounters with all statuses including Review Not Needed. When the Discharge Date is applied, then this card displays the count of discharged encounters with all statuses including Review Not Needed.

conbody.p: This chart displays the encounter level details for each encounter.

Info Icon(Text on hover): Encounter Status: Includes all encounter statuses except 'Review Not Needed.' For data on those encounters, use the 'Encounter Status' filter from the Filters pane. The visual shows the data for all ranked (standard/custom) and unranked encounters by default. For data on encounters with specific ranks, use the 'Highest Rank' or 'Current Rank' slicer.

row.entry:
Displays the facility assigned to the encounter in the format Facility Description (Facility ID).
Example: Good Health Hospital (GHH).

Displays the Account Number assigned to the encounter.
Example: ACC2021001.

Displays the MRN assigned to the encounter.
Example: MRN2021001.

Displays the name of the patient associated with the encounter.
Example: Last Name, First Name.

entry.p: Displays the Admission Date and Time assigned to the encounter in the facility's timezone.
Displays the discharge date and time assigned to the encounter in the facility's timezone.
This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a discharge date assigned.
Displays the CDS assigned to the encounter in the format Last Name, First Name MI (CDS ID).
Example: Ryan, Melissa (rmelissa).
Displays the date and time when the initial review was performed on the encounter. The date is displayed in the Facility's time-zone. Example: 05/24/2020 10:10:00 AM ET.

p.note: If the Initial Review Date/time is after the Discharge Date/Time, Initial Review Date/Time is displayed in the red font.

entry.p: Displays the name of the CDS who performed the initial review.
Example: Ryan, Melissa (rmelissa).
This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Initial Review Date/Time'.

row.entry:
Displays the length of stay in days for each encounter.
LOS value is displayed in blue font, when LOS is greater than GMLOS for the encounter.
W-GMLOS value is considered, if F-GMLOS value is blank.

entry.p: Displays the standard or custom rank assigned to the encounter prior to the Initial Review. E.g. 1, 2, etc.
This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Rank at Initial Review' assigned.
Displays the highest rank (standard or custom) assigned to the encounter (1 being the highest, and 11 being the lowest).
This column is blank for the unranked encounters, i.e. encounters that never had a rank assigned in CDE Triage.

row.entry:
Displays the current standard or custom rank assigned to the encounter. E.g. 1, 2, etc.
This column is blank for the unranked encounters, i.e. encounters that never had a rank assigned in CDE Triage.

entry.p: Displays the tracking codes assigned to the encounter in comma separated format.
Example: Quality Review, Prov Education.
Displays the Suggested DRG Code and its description.
Example: 872 - SEPTICEMIA OR SEVERE SEPSIS WITHOUT MV >96 HOURS WITHOUT MAJOR COMPLICATION OR COMORBIDITY (MCC).
Displays the 'primary' Working DRG along with the description displayed on the Review page.
Example: 872 - SEPTICEMIA OR SEVERE SEPSIS WITHOUT MV >96 HOURS WITHOUT MAJOR COMPLICATION OR COMORBIDITY (MCC).
Displays the WT associated with the 'primary' Working DRG displayed on the Review page.
Example: 1.0393.
Displays the 'SOI' from the working review.
Example: 2.

p.note: If the SOI associated with the primary working DRG is not available, then the SOI associated with the advanced working DRG is displayed.

entry.p: Displays the 'ROM' from the working review.
Example: 3.

p.note: If the ROM associated with the primary working DRG is not available, then the ROM associated with the advanced working DRG is displayed.

entry.p: Displays the 'AMLOS' from the working review.
Example: 3.1.

p.note: If the AMLOS associated with the primary working DRG is not available, then the AMLOS associated with the advanced working DRG is displayed.

entry.p: Displays the 'GMLOS ' from the working review.
Example: 3.2.

p.note: If the GMLOS associated with the primary working DRG is not available, then the GMLOS associated with the advanced working DRG is displayed.

entry.p: Displays the 'primary' Possible DRG along with the description displayed on the Review page.
Example: 872 - SEPTICEMIA OR SEVERE SEPSIS WITHOUT MV >96 HOURS WITHOUT MAJOR COMPLICATION OR COMORBIDITY (MCC).

p.note: Displays the ‘Working DRG’ value in the orange font for the encounters that have a ‘Working DRG’ assigned if the ‘Possible DRG’ is blank or null.

entry.p: Displays the WT associated with the 'primary' Possible DRG displayed on the Review page.
Example: 1.0393.

p.note: Displays the ‘Working DRG WT’ value in the orange font for the encounters that have a ‘Working DRG’ assigned if the ‘Possible DRG’ is blank or null.

entry.p: Displays the code and description of the Reconciliation Assessment DRG along with the description displayed on the Reconciliation page.
Example: 872 - SEPTICEMIA OR SEVERE SEPSIS WITHOUT MV >96 HOURS WITHOUT MAJOR COMPLICATION OR COMORBIDITY (MCC).
Displays the WT associated with the Reconciliation Assessment DRG displayed on the Reconciliation page.
Example: 1.0393.
Displays the 'SOI' from the Reconciliation Assessment.
Example: 1

p.note: If the SOI associated with the primary Reconciliation Assessment DRG is not available, then the SOI associated with the advanced Reconciliation Assessment DRG is displayed.

entry.p: This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Initial Review Date/Time'.
Displays the 'ROM' from the Reconciliation Assessment.
Example: 1

p.note: If the ROM associated with the primary Reconciliation Assessment DRG is not available, then the ROM associated with the advanced Reconciliation Assessment DRG is displayed.

entry.p: This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Initial Review Date/Time'
Displays the 'AMLOS' from the Reconciliation Assessment.
Example: 3.1

p.note: If the AMLOS associated with the primary Reconciliation Assessment DRG is not available, then the AMLOS associated with the advanced Reconciliation Assessment DRG is displayed.

entry.p: This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Initial Review Date/Time'.
Displays the 'GMLOS' from the Reconciliation Assessment.
Example: 2.4

p.note: If the GMLOS associated with the primary Reconciliation Assessment DRG is not available, then the GMLOS associated with the advanced Reconciliation Assessment DRG is displayed.

entry.p: This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Initial Review Date/Time'
Displays the code and description of the Final Coded DRG along with the description displayed on the Reconciliation page.
Example: 872 - SEPTICEMIA OR SEVERE SEPSIS WITHOUT MV >96 HOURS WITHOUT MAJOR COMPLICATION OR COMORBIDITY (MCC).
entry.note: This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Initial Review Date/Time'.

entry.p: Displays the WT associated with the Final Coded DRG displayed on the Reconciliation page.
Example: 1.0393.

p.note: This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Initial Review Date/Time

entry.p: Displays the Final Coded 'SOI'.
Example: 2
This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Initial Review Date/Time'
Advanced SOI values are used if Primary SOI values are blank.
Displays the Final Coded 'ROM'.
Example: 3.
This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Initial Review Date/Time'
Advanced ROM values are used if Primary ROM values are blank.
Displays the Final Coded 'AMLOS'.
Example: 3.1.
This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Initial Review Date/Time'
Advanced AMLOS values are used if Primary AMLOS values are blank.
Displays the Final Coded 'GMLOS'.
Example: 3.2
This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Initial Review Date/Time'
Advanced GMLOS values are used if Primary GMLOS values are blank
Displays the shift in the WT at the encounter level.
Example: 1.0178.

Encounter Variance = Final Coded DRG WT - Reconciliation Assessment DRG WT (or Variance = Final Coded DRG WT - Working DRG WT when Discharge Date < 11/14/19 or Reconciliation Assessment DRG is blank). For encounters with blank Final DRG WT, Encounter Variance = Possible DRG WT - Working DRG WT ( If Possible DRG/WT is blank, Working DRG WT is considered. In this case Encounter Variance will always be zero).

ul.li: Encounter Variance can be negative, positive, or zero.
Negative ‘Variance’ is displayed in the red font, E.g. 1.0178.
Encounter Variance is not calculated for the encounters that do not have an initial review date.
This value is rounded to 4 decimal places.

entry.p: Displays the Blended Rate associated with the payer/Grouper assigned to the encounter.
Example: $7512.
When the Blended Rate is not available, the Operating Federal rate is displayed.
Displays the ‘Reimbursement Impact’ value for the encounter.
Example: $ 5,234.
Reimbursement Impact: Encounter Reimbursement Impact = Encounter Variance x Blended Rate.

ul.li: This value is rounded to 0 decimal places.
Negative ‘Reimbursement Impact ($)’ is displayed in the red font.

entry.p: Displays the Impact Type assigned to the encounter:

ul.li: Financial Impact
SOI/ROM Impact
No Impact
Both Impact
Unassigned Impact

row.entry:
Displays the encounter status assigned to the encounter.
Example: Reconciled Impact, Auto-Reconciled..

Displays the payer assigned to the encounter.
Example: Medicare (MEDC).

Displays the Grouper version associated with the payer assigned to the encounter.
Example: CMS 38.0.

Displays the Unit assigned to the encounter.
Example: ICU.

Displays the Patient Type assigned to the encounter.
Example: Inpatient

entry.p: Displays the Visit Type assigned to the encounter.

row.entry:
Displays the Discharge Status assigned to the encounter.
Format: Discharge Status ID- Description Status Description.
Example: 01 - 01 Self Care.

conbody.p: This chart displays the suggestion level details for each encounter.

Info Icon(Text on hover): Encounter Status: Includes data for all encounter statuses except ‘Review Not Needed.' For data on those encounters, use the 'Encounter Status' filter from the Filters pane. The visual shows suggestions for all ranked (standard/custom) and unranked encounters by default. For suggestion data on encounters with specific ranks, use the 'Highest Rank' or 'Current Rank' slicer and then select the encounter from the above 'Encounter Details’ chart.

row.entry:
Displays the facility assigned to the encounter in the format Facility Description (Facility ID).
Example: Good Health Hospital (GHH).

Displays The Account Number assigned to the encounter.
Example: ACC2021001.

entry.p: Displays the Admission Date and Time assigned to the encounter in the facility's timezone.
Displays the discharge date and time assigned to the encounter in the facility's timezone.
This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a discharge date assigned.
Displays displays one of the below values:
This column is blank for an encounter for which no AI suggestions were presented in CDS Assistant.
Displays one of the below values:
The column is blank for an encounter for which no AI suggestions were presented in CDS Assistant.

row.entry:
Displays the AI suggestion that was presented in CDS Assistant.
When Assistant Category is Documented Diagnoses, this column displays the diagnosis in the format ICD 10 Code: Diagnoses Description (E.g. L97.909: Non pressure chronic ulcer of unspecified part of unspecified leg with unspecified severity).
When Assistant Category is Documented Procedures, this column displays the procedures in the format ICD10 PCS code: Procedure Description or SNOMED Procedure Description (E.g. 0RG2: Fusion cervical vertebral joints, Debridement of wound of skin).
When Assistant Category is Documentation Opportunities Diagnoses, this column displays the diagnosis in the format ICD 10 Code: Diagnoses Description (E.g., N17.9: Acute kidney failure, unspecified).
When Assistant Category is Documentation Opportunities Clinical Evidences, this column displays the evidence in the format Clinical Evidence Description (E.g., Gram-Negative Blood Culture).The column is blank for an encounter for which no AI suggestions were presented in CDS Assistant.

entry.p: Displays the flag assigned to the AI suggestion. (E.g. PSI, CC, MCC, ELX, HAC, HCC, OR, NOR, etc.)
Flags are displayed for Documented Diagnoses, Documented Procedures, and Documentation Opportunities Diagnoses.

p.note: This column will be blank for Documentation Opportunities : Clinical Evidences.

entry.p: An AI suggestion may have more than one flag assigned. For example, Documented Diagnoses N93.0: Urinary tract infection, site not specified, may have two flags - CC and HAC assigned as applicable to the encounter. In case of multiple flags, the values are displayed in comma separated format.
The column will be blank for an encounter for which no AI suggestions were presented in CDS Assistant.
Displays one of the below values:
The column is blank for an encounter for which no AI suggestions were presented in CDS Assistant.

row.entry:
Displays the encounter status assigned to the encounter.
Example: Reconciled Impact, Auto-Reconciled..

Displays The Payer assigned to the encounter in the format Payer Description (Payer ID).
Example: Medicare (MEDC).

Displays the Unit assigned to the encounter.
Example: ICU.

Displays the Patient Type assigned to the encounter.
Example: Inpatient

entry.p: Displays the Visit Type assigned to the encounter.

row.entry:
Displays the Discharge Status assigned to the encounter.
Format: Discharge Status ID- Description Status Description.
Example: 01 - 01 Self Care.

li.p: Allows the user to select the admit date range.
Default: Last 1 calendar month date range is selected by default.

ul.li: Display the discharge date range.
With the Reset button, you can reset both the admit and discharge date range.
Tooltip: Ctrl + Click to reset both Admit Date and Discharge Date.
Displays both active and inactive facilities in alphabetical order that you have access to.
You can select multiple facilities.
You can search the facility by Facility ID or Description.
Format: Facility Description (Facility ID).
Default: ALL.
Displays both active and inactive payers in alphabetical order associated with the facilities you have access to.
You can select multiple payers.
"Unassigned Payer" displays data for the encounters that do not have a payer assigned.
You can search the Payer by Payer ID or Description.
Format: Payer Description (Payer ID).
Default: ALL.

li.p: Displays both active and inactive CDSs in alphabetical order associated with the facilities user has access to.
"Unassigned CDS" displays data for the encounters that do not have a CDS assigned.
Users can search the CDS by CDS ID or Name.

ul.li: This filter displays the list of available units.
You can select multiple Units.
You can search the Unit.
Default: ALL.

row.entry:
Displays the below options:
Documented Diagnoses
Documented Procedures
DO: Diagnoses
DO: Clinical Evidence
User can select multiple Assistant categories.
Default: Documented Diagnoses and DO: Diagnoses.

Displays the below options:
Accepted
Removed
Unaddressed
CDS Attributed
System Attributed
User can select multiple activities.
Default: ALL.

Displays the below options:
Suggested
Quality
Other
User can select multiple suggestion types.
Default: Suggested.

li.p: Displays the list of Documented Diagnoses, Documented Procedures, Documentation Opportunities Diagnoses, Documentation Opportunities Clinical Evidences.
User can select multiple diagnoses, procedures or evidences.
Users can search a diagnosis/procedure/evidence by ICD 10 Code or Diagnoses/Procedure/Evidences Description.

Format: ICD 10 Code: Diagnoses Description (E.g. L97.909: Non pressure chronic ulcer of unspecified part of unspecified leg with unspecified severity), Procedure Description (E.g., Debridement of wound of skin), Clinical Evidence Description (E.g., Gram-Negative Blood Culture). Only display the first 20-25 characters, including the ICD-10 Code and show the rest of the description in a tooltip upon hover.

Displays all standard and custom ranks that can be assigned to the encounters. This slicer filters the data as per the highest standard/custom rank that has ever been assigned to an encounter (1 being the highest standard rank and 11 being the lowest standard rank).
Users can search the Rank number.
“Unranked” displays data for all encounters that are not ranked.
Displays all standard and custom ranks that can be assigned to the encounters. This slicer filters the data as per the current standard or custom rank assigned to an encounter.
Users can search the Rank number.
“Unranked” displays data for all encounters that are not ranked.

ul.li: Displays the available patient types. Example: Inpatient, Outpatient.
Default: Inpatient.
Displays the list of available admit service.
You can select multiple Units.
You can search the Admit Service.
Default: ALL
Displays the available patient types.Example: ED, IP, etc.
You can select multiple Visit Type.
You can search the Visit Type.
Default: ALL.

li.p: Displays the available options for Weekend Only Stay- Y and N.
Weekend Only Stay = Y (Unassigned encounters admitted after Friday, 6PM and discharged before Sunday, 11.59 PM).

ul.li: Default: ALL..

li.p: Displays the list of available encounter statuses.
Users can search the Encounter Status.
Default: 'Review Not Needed' unchecked by default.

row.entry: Displays all the AI suggested primary and secondary, manually added, and ‘Removed' diagnosis codes presented in the 'Documented: Diagnoses’ section within CDS Assistant.
Displays all the AI suggested, manually added, and 'Removed' procedure codes (ICD 10 PCS and SNOMED) presented in the 'Documented: Procedures’ section within CDS Assistant.
Displays all the AI suggested diagnoses presented in the Documentation Opportunities: Diagnoses section within CDS Assistant.

Displays all the signs, symptoms, medications, lab results or procedures found within a provider document. Clinical Evidences are presented in the 'Documentation Opportunities: Clinical Evidences’ section within CDS Assistant.
Displays Initial Review Date/time in red font when the Initial Review Date/Time > Discharge Date/Time.
Displays Length of Stay in blue font if LOS value greater than F-GMLOS; W-GMLOS value is considered in case of blank F-GMLOS.

entry.p: P-DRG: Displays the ‘Working DRG’ value in orange font for the reconciled encounters with blank ‘Possible DRG’.
P-DRG WT: Displays the ‘Working DRG WT’ value in orange font for the reconciled encounters with blank ‘Possible DRG’.

row.entry: Variance = Final Coded DRG WT - Reconciliation Assessment DRG WT (Working DRG WT when Discharge Date < 11/14/19 or Reconciliation Assessment DRG is blank); Displays negative variance value in red font.
Configured at payer level; When Blended Rate is not available, Operating Federal rate is displayed.

entry.p: Reimbursement Impact = (Final Coded DRG WT - Reconciliation Assessment DRG WT (Working DRG WT when Discharge Date < 11/14/19 or Reconciliation Assessment DRG is blank)) x Blended Rate. E.g. $5321
Displays negative Reimbursement Impact in red font.

row.entry: Advanced DRG SOI/ROM value is displayed when Primary DRG SOI/ROM is blank.
Advanced DRG AMLOS/GMLOS value is displayed when Primary DRG AMLOS/GMLOS is blank.
